intro: Canscorinella bhatiana is a medicinal plant species, belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly found in the Western Ghats of India. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, inflammation, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Canscorinella bhatiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Canscorinella bhatiana is often used to prepare various herbal formulations, including decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
